ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-29-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross former Attorney for Jaimie Klee. Andrew Schultz for Gene Marchi (Administrator). Gerhard Stoll for Jaimie Klee (Remiaining Beneficiary).

Mr. Stoll stated that there was an issue with the Administrator's fees. The Court stated that there was no problem with the extraordinary fees, and ordered the ordinary fees be divided 10% to Roger Gross and 90% to the Adminstrator's attorney. Mr. Schultz is to submit the order. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment And Supporting Memorandum
Off Calendar Oct-25-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Gerhard Stoll appeared for Jaime Klee (Petitioner/ Respondent). No other appearances were made.

The Court questioned Mr. Stoll about notification to opposing counsel. Mr. Stoll stated that he was not aware of the tentative ruling and did not give notice. The Court informed Mr. Stoll that he was required to notify opposing Counsel. Therefore, the court could not hear arguement on the merit. The tentative ruling became the final order of the Court. The motion was denied. Mr. Stoll stated that he intends to file a motion for reconsideration.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-10-2007 Continued To Jan-31-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Gerhard Stoll (Counsel for Jaime Klee, Petitioner); Jon Vaught (counsel for Martha Kolstad and Calvin York, Objectors).

Counsel advised the Court of the status of the case, the will contest, the possibility of Gene Marchi serving as special administrator of the estate. Based upon counsels' representations, the Court continued the matter to January 31, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. However, if the parties agree on the appointment of Mr. Marchi as special administrator, counsel can present the same by way of ex parte application and the January 31, 2007 hearing will be taken off calendar.

A status hearing for the will contest will be set for February 28, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judicial Officer: Judge John Dearman; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Court Examiner: Tim Kneis; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
